channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: ElevenLabs lists BlackRock, Jamie Foxx and Longoria as new investors
item.description: 4. ElevenLabs reveals new investors, hits $500M ARR, and expands enterprise footprint as voice AI becomes a critical interface.

item.title: Audio-to-haptics: perfectly syncing phone vibration to audio on the web — how I made it
item.description: The circle fills and pulses in sync with the audio — this is what your phone is feeling. The GIF shows it, but you won't really get it until you feel it. Open this on Android and try it yourself →...

item.title: Why Automation is the Secret Weapon of Top Developers in 2026
item.description: What’s up, Dev Community! 👋 We’re living in 2026, and the role of a developer has evolved. It’s no longer just about grinding through syntax or writing thousands of lines of code; it’s about how...

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: AI Workflow Automation Tools Are a Mess, Here’s What I Learned the Hard Way VK VK VK Follow for Composio May 5 AI Workflow Automation Tools Are a Mess, Here’s What I Learned the Hard Way #ai...

item.title: AI didn't delete your database, you did
item.description: Article URL: https://idiallo.com/blog/ai-didnt-delete-your-database-you-did Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48022742 Points: 14 # Comments: 1

item.title: CopilotKit raises $27M to help devs deploy app-native AI agents
item.description: The Seattle-based startup's Series A round was led by Glilot Capital, NFX and SignalFire, TechCrunch has exclusively learned.

item.title: 80% of the time, coding is a performance. The other 20% is doing real coding.
item.description: Coding For A Living Is More Performance Than Code Cesar Aguirre Cesar Aguirre Cesar Aguirre Follow May 4 Coding For A Living Is More Performance Than Code #discuss #coding #beginners #career 1...

item.title: New Mexico has a plan to overhaul Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Still fresh off its recent $375 million jury verdict against Meta, New Mexico attorney general Raul Torrez's office began arguing for even greater asks in the second phase of a landmark trial. On...

item.title: I Built Watchup — A Simple Way to Track and Monitor What Matters on the Web
item.description: I Built Watchup — An African Alternative to Sentry for Monitoring Services Most developers don’t realize their app is down until users complain. By then, the damage is already done. That’s the...

item.title: Kubernetes CronJobs silently fail more than you think
item.description: A backup job missed 24 days of runs. Nobody knew. The CronJob looked fine in kubectl get cronjobs. No alerts fired. The last successful run timestamp in the status field just sat there, quietly...

item.title: Me, myself and AI
item.description: I am currently working with the EA on their check for flooding team. I have been tasked to look at the 5 day river level charts with a view to add more historical data. This meant increasing the...

item.title: Stop triaging Go CVEs that don't affect you
item.description: If you maintain Go services, you've probably been here: a scanner flags a CVE, you spend 30 minutes tracing imports and call paths, and it turns out your code never touches the vulnerable...

item.title: How I cut AI calls by 95% without losing quality?
item.description: The Hidden Cost of Calling AI Too Early I stopped calling AI on every request — and everything got better. The Problem In one of my projects, I was generating AI-based insights from user activity....

item.title: 4 days left: Get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass to make more deals faster
item.description: For the next four days only, you can buy one pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get 50% off a second of the same ticket type. That window closes May 8 at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices go up,...

item.title: Zombies, Run! is officially back from the dead
item.description: Starting tomorrow, Zombies, Run! fans will be able to download a new eight-part story for the fitness app. That's nothing short of a miracle. In 2012, Zombies, Run! was one of the first fitness...

item.title: What an AI-designed car looks like
item.description: The cars rolling off production lines right now are filled with old ideas. From beginning to end, the creation of a new vehicle can take five years or longer - which is plenty of time for a lot of...

item.title: Remedy’s new boss says the Control studio will ‘double down’ on what makes it unique
item.description: It's a big year for Remedy Entertainment. Last year was marred by the ill-fated launch of the multiplayer shooter FBC: Firebreak, which was so disastrous it led to former CEO Tero Virtala stepping...

item.title: India’s first GenAI unicorn shifts to cloud services as AI model ambitions face reality
item.description: Krutrim's pivot to cloud after layoffs and limited product updates reflects the economic challenges of building AI models in India.

item.title: Researchers gaslit Claude into giving instructions to build explosives
item.description: Anthropic has spent years building itself up as the safe AI company. But new security research shared with The Verge suggests Claude's carefully crafted helpful personality may itself be a...

item.title: Google’s AI architect lived rent-free in Elon Musk’s head
item.description: About a week into the Musk v. Altman trial, we've heard from some of the most powerful people in tech - including OpenAI president Greg Brockman, Elon Musk's fixer Jared Birchall, and Musk...

item.title: Inside the lab where Ford is trying to crack the code on cheap EVs
item.description: Ford isn't sending the clearest signals regarding its EV future. We got a glimpse of its Universal EV (UEV) Project last February, but then it pulled the plug on the F-150 Lightning, which is...

item.title: The Download: inside the Musk v. Altman trial, and AI for democracy
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: what it was like in...

item.title: Bose takes a swing at Sonos with its new home speakers
item.description: Last Thursday, in a staged home on the Upper West Side of Manhattan, I sat on a couch that was a bit too low and a tad too deep in front of a small, beige-colored smart speaker sitting on the...

item.title: Google DeepMind Workers Vote to Unionize Over Military AI Deals
item.description: UK staff of Google's AI research lab hope to block the use of the company's artificial intelligence models in military settings.

item.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
item.description: Every AI tool, workflow automation, and productivity app your employees connected to Google or Microsoft this year left something behind: a persistent OAuth token with no expiration date, no...

item.title: MetInfo CMS CVE-2026-29014 Exploited for Remote Code Execution Attacks
item.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw impacting an open-source content management system (CMS) known as MetInfo, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The...

item.title: Why Reddit blocked my daily visit to its mobile website
item.description: Reddit REALLY wants you to use its app.

item.title: Facebook and Instagram are using AI bone structure analysis to identify photos of kids
item.description: Facebook and Instagram have a new way to detect and remove users under 13: AI bone structure analysis. In a blog post on Tuesday, Meta - Facebook and Instagram's parent company - says its AI...

item.title: DarkSword Malware
item.description: DarkSword is a sophisticated piece of malware—probably government designed—that targets iOS. Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified a new iOS full-chain exploit that leveraged...

item.title: We Scanned 1 Million Exposed AI Services. Here's How Bad the Security Actually Is
item.description: While the software industry has made genuine strides over the past few decades to deliver products securely, the furious pace of AI adoption is putting that progress at risk. Businesses are moving...
